forms.definition: To make a choice after thinking
A choice that is made after thinking
Showing the ability to make clear decisions
In a way that settles an issue clearly

forms.example: He was indecisive about the move.
Scientists analyze data to find patterns.

families.etymology: Old French: achever (come to a head)

forms.definition: To succeed in doing something difficult
forms.example: She achieved her goal after years of work.
Winning the award was a great achievement.

forms.definition: A person who achieves a lot
forms.example: He's a high achiever in his field.

forms.definition: To make or produce something new
The act of creating or something created
forms.example: The creation of the universe is a mystery.

forms.definition: The ability to create new ideas
forms.example: Creativity is valued in the workplace.
The creator of the app won an award.

forms.definition: To grow or make something grow
forms.example: The company developed a new product.

forms.definition: The process of growing or improving
To set up or create something officially
The process of setting up or a business
forms.example: The establishment of a school took years.

forms.definition: Well-known and accepted for a long time
The act of identifying or proof of identity
forms.example: Show your identification at the door.

forms.definition: Who or what a person or thing is
To show or point to something
forms.example: The data indicates a positive trend.
High sales are indicative of success.

forms.definition: To keep something in good condition
The act of keeping something in good condition
Able to be kept in good condition
The act of providing or a supply
forms.example: Food provision is critical in a crisis.

forms.definition: A person or thing that provides
To propose an idea for consideration
forms.example: Can you suggest a good restaurant?

forms.definition: Tending to suggest or imply something
forms.example: The results are suggestive of progress.

forms.definition: To include or require as a part
The fact of taking part in something
Engaged in an activity or situation
To happen or come to mind

forms.example: A recurring dream woke her up.

forms.definition: Feeling or showing pleasure and contentment
forms.example: I am happy to see you.
She felt unhappy about the result.
She showed great strength of character.

forms.definition: To make it easier for someone to do something
forms.example: Can you help me with this?
We recognize the importance of health.

forms.definition: Able to be done or achieved
forms.example: Is it possible to finish today?
Nothing is impossible if you try.

forms.definition: To put into service or employ
forms.example: Use a pen to sign your name.

forms.definition: Able to be used for a practical purpose
forms.example: A knife is a useful tool.
Time can be usefully spent reading.
Usefulness is the key to good design.

forms.definition: In a way that produces results

forms.example: Measure the effectiveness of a plan.

forms.definition: A change produced by a cause
forms.example: The drug had a calming effect.

forms.definition: To have an effect on something
To say or do something in reply
Having a duty to deal with something

forms.example: It was irresponsible to skip work.

forms.definition: In an important or meaningful way

forms.example: The significance of the event grew.

forms.definition: Willing to talk and share information
Large or important in size or amount
forms.example: A considerable amount of work remains.

forms.definition: Careful not to hurt others' feelings
The surroundings or conditions for life
A person who protects the environment

forms.example: Global warming is a serious issue.

forms.definition: The system of production and wealth
forms.example: The economy grew by 3% last year.

forms.definition: The study of production and wealth
To find an answer or solution
forms.example: Can you solve this math problem?

forms.definition: A means of solving a problem
forms.example: We found a solution to the issue.

forms.definition: To gain an advantage from something
forms.example: You will benefit from reading more.

forms.definition: A person who benefits from something
forms.example: She is the beneficiary of the will.

forms.definition: To say what will happen in the future
A statement about what will happen

forms.example: Friends support you in hard times.

forms.definition: To introduce new ideas or methods
A new idea or method introduced
To connect or have a relationship
forms.example: Can you relate to this feeling?
There is a relation between diet and health.

forms.definition: The way two things are connected
forms.example: A good relationship is built on trust.

forms.definition: Considered in comparison to something else
forms.example: It's all relative to your experience.
The company plans to expand overseas.

forms.definition: Covering a wide area or range
forms.example: An expansive garden with many flowers.

families.etymology: Latin: assessare (to sit beside as judge)

forms.example: She spoke relevantly on the topic.
The relevance of history is clear.

forms.definition: Showing a great deal of variety
forms.example: The city has a diverse population.
Are you aware of the risks?
She was unaware of the problem.

forms.definition: To accept as true without proof
forms.example: Don't assume you know the answer.

forms.definition: A thing accepted as true without proof

forms.example: He was assuming in his behavior.

forms.definition: A sum of money saved or collected
To collect money for a cause
forms.example: They fundraise for charity every year.

forms.definition: A person or event that collects funds
To give something as part of a whole
Something given as part of a whole
Playing a part in causing something
To put a plan or decision into effect
forms.example: We will implement the changes soon.

forms.definition: The process of putting something into effect

forms.example: Work hard and you will succeed.

forms.definition: The achievement of a desired result
To be determined or influenced by
The state of depending on something
To put something to use or request formally

forms.example: This rule is applicable to everyone.

forms.definition: A person who applies for something
forms.example: There were many applicants for the role.

forms.definition: To try to win against others
Having a strong desire to win
A person or company that competes
forms.example: Our main competitor launched a product.

forms.definition: To give knowledge or training to
To give reasons for or against something
forms.example: Lawyers argue their case in court.

forms.definition: A reason given for a viewpoint

forms.example: Her influence in the field is great.

forms.definition: To have an effect on something

forms.example: They promoted the new product online.

forms.definition: The act of promoting or advancement
forms.example: She got a promotion at work.

forms.definition: A plan designed to achieve a goal
To explain the meaning of something
forms.example: How do you interpret this poem?

Reliability is key to customer trust.

forms.definition: To join or link things together
forms.example: Connect the cable to the device.

forms.definition: A link between things or people

forms.example: I feel disconnected from my roots.

forms.definition: A device or thing that connects

forms.example: Organize your desk to work better.

forms.definition: A group with a common purpose; orderly arrangement
forms.example: She works for a non-profit organization.

A disorganized workspace slows you down.
